name: Enimiro Uganda
existing_debt: CFC (undisclosed); Rabo Foundation (undisclosed); One Acre Ventures ($250K–$750K, 2025); Pearl Capital $515K equity (exited 2024)
capital_use_of_funds: Vanilla working capital; coffee processing expansion
crs_notes: CRS directly supported Enimiro lab setup via VINES project. Pearl exit means equity slot open; debt is natural next instrument. One Acre Ventures just entered for coffee — CRS facility should focus on vanilla working capital to avoid overlap.
notes: Post-Pearl exit, One Acre Ventures entering for coffee. 80% female staff. EUDR traceability in place. Clearest near-term deal in Uganda. CRS lab support via VINES is a strong relationship anchor.

name: Natural Extracts Industries (NEI)
capital_use_of_funds: Uganda-specific working capital; vanilla extraction scale-up
crs_notes: NEI received VINES VIEF grant for farmer training, registration, certification and processing facility improvement. Joris de Vries on board — key relationship to activate. Any facility must ring-fence Uganda operations from Tanzania parent.
notes: Most sophisticated operator in East Africa vanilla extract space. Uganda is a subset of Tanzania-anchored business — isolate Uganda entity financials before advancing. Feb 2026: partnered with Velora Enterprises (uga-013) for premium Ugandan vanilla supply chain.

name: Esco Uganda Ltd
capital_use_of_funds: Seasonal crop purchase working capital
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES. Largest and oldest vanilla exporter in Uganda. At 150+ staff and 20,000 farmers likely above SME ceiling. Track as sector anchor and referral source. Notably absent from current VANEX membership despite being a founding member.
notes: SME classification caveat: 150+ staff, 20,000 farmers. Revenue estimate could be significantly higher. No longer listed as VANEX member despite being a founding member — reason unknown.

name: Rajon Vanilla Limited
existing_debt: None identified (no funding rounds per Tracxn)
capital_use_of_funds: Working capital for crop aggregation; certification costs; processing equipment
crs_notes: Strong livelihoods narrative — healthcare initiative, education programmes, 50,000+ indirect beneficiaries claimed. Western Uganda focus is high CRS livelihoods relevance. Likely needs TA or certification support before debt is appropriate.
notes: Co-founder Rajiv Ruparelia linked to Ruparelia Group (one of Uganda's largest conglomerates) — may signal capital access or that external debt is not a priority. No certifications confirmed. Not a VANEX member or VIEF partner.

name: Natural Extracts Industries Uganda Ltd (NEI-U)
farmer_network_size: Part of NEI's 13,000+ network — Uganda share unknown
capital_use_of_funds: Unknown
crs_notes: Listed separately in VANEX as a distinct Uganda entity from Tanzania NEI parent. VIEF partner via VINES. May be the better entry point for a Uganda-specific facility. Needs clarification on whether Uganda entity has independent financials.
notes: Consider merging with uga-002 unless Uganda financials can be isolated. Direct VANEX contact: amulindwa@gmail.com, +256752870289.

name: Gourmet Gardens
existing_debt: AGEFA (Uganda Green Enterprise Finance Accelerator) — amount undisclosed
capital_use_of_funds: Processing capacity; farmer network expansion
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES. AGEFA-backed (EU-funded green enterprise accelerator). Based in Rwenzori Mountains — same cluster as Tambiisa and Coetzee Organic. Featured in US Embassy Uganda N*Gen episode alongside Enimiro.
notes: Limited public profile beyond AGEFA listing. Rwenzori cluster creates opportunities for cluster-based lending approach alongside Tambiisa and Coetzee. Direct contact not yet identified.

name: Coetzee Organic (U) Ltd
farmer_network_size: Multiple formalised smallholder farmer groups (size unknown); FLAVEX partnership: 154 farmers across 3 cooperatives
existing_debt: FLAVEX Naturextrakte GmbH (German buyer-partner pre-season financing model)
capital_use_of_funds: Pre-season farmer financing; farmer security costs; processing equipment
capital_need_source: Claude estimate — VANEX profile notes pre-season farmer security financing as active need; FLAVEX partnership confirms working capital gap
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES. VANEX member. First Uganda company to introduce quality-based pricing combined with Fairtrade and Organic premiums. FLAVEX (Germany) co-finances pre-season farmer security costs — this is an explicit working capital use case CRS could formalise. Five-certification stack is exceptional for a smaller operator.
notes: Pre-season farmer security financing (armed guards for vanilla crops) is a concrete, documentable working capital need. Managing Director contact is South African-registered — company may be expat-led. FLAVEX partnership with Rwenzori cooperatives is well documented.

name: Tambiisa (U) Ltd
farmer_network_size: Hundreds of smallholder organic farmers (Rwenzori area)
capital_use_of_funds: Seasonal crop purchase working capital; processing scale-up
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES. VANEX member. One of the more established Ugandan-owned vanilla exporters operating since 2008. Rwenzori sourcing geography. State-of-the-art factory in Wakiso district near Kampala.
notes: Direct CEO contact available via VANEX directory. Ugandan-owned and operated since 2008 — stronger ownership profile than some expat-led peers. No public financial information available.

name: Rwenzori Farmers' Cooperative Union (RFCU)
hq_location: Rwenzori Mountains foothills, Kasese district, western Uganda
capital_use_of_funds: Pre-harvest working capital; farmer input finance; collective processing
capital_need_source: Claude estimate — cooperative model with Fairtrade premium redistribution suggests strong governance but limited commercial credit access
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES. First Fairtrade vanilla cooperative on the African continent (2005). Primary buyer is Ndali Estate (UK) — long-term exclusive relationship. CRS engagement must be complementary. High livelihoods impact — Fairtrade premiums fund community pharmacy, school roofing, scholarships, maize milling.
notes: Exceptional community investment track record. Direct contact details not publicly available beyond Ndali documentation. Would benefit from TA support before direct debt. Ndali Estate relationship is both an asset (market access) and a constraint (exclusivity).

name: Sagetra (U) Ltd
capital_use_of_funds: Unknown
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES — confirmed by CRS source. No public web presence or VANEX listing found. CRS VINES team is the primary route to contact information.
notes: Placeholder entry — no public information available. Contact via CRS VINES team: David Hirst (Chief of Party) — david.hirst@crs.org, +256 757 288356.

name: Touton (U) Ltd
hq_location: Kasese district, Rwenzori region, western Uganda
org_type: Subsidiary of large French trading house
revenue_usd_est: Not applicable (subsidiary of major trading house)
capital_use_of_funds: N/A — backed by large parent
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES. Touton is a 170-year-old French commodity trading house — Uganda subsidiary is not a lending target. Track as sector intelligence and potential buyer-partner. Rwenzori regenerative agriculture programme noteworthy for environment alignment.
sme_classification_note: Not an SME — subsidiary of large trading house
notes: Not a lending target. Included for sector completeness as a VIEF partner and major Rwenzori value chain actor. Could be useful as a market linkage partner or buyer reference.

name: AMFRI Farms Ltd (African Organic)
hq_location: Katalemwa Estate, Kampala (farms across central and northern Uganda)
existing_debt: Yield Fund Uganda (Pearl Capital) $1.3M joint investment; FCA Investments (equity, undisclosed)
capital_use_of_funds: Seasonal spice and fruit working capital; processing expansion
capital_need_source: Claude estimate — Yield Fund investment was for processing scale-up; ongoing seasonal working capital is likely the residual need
crs_notes: No confirmed CRS link. Strong alignment: biodynamic/organic farming, Fair For Life, Demeter — only such plantation on the equatorial belt globally. Spices (vanilla, ginger, turmeric, chilli, herbs) are a major product line. Yield Fund investment implies audited accounts exist. 40% women employees.
sme_classification_note: Upper end of SME — 473 staff; assess fit
notes: At 473 staff may be above SME ceiling, but Yield Fund invested suggesting SME-eligible at time. First certified organic producer in Uganda. New 500-acre Mabira forest rehabilitation/spice project is a strong environment story.

name: Velora Enterprises
capital_use_of_funds: Working capital for vanilla aggregation and processing
capital_need_source: Claude estimate — new entrant; working capital is primary scaling constraint
crs_notes: Very new operator — first major transaction (5 tons vanilla with NEI) announced February 2026. Sources from Bundibugyo and Central Uganda, including DRC cross-border. NEI partnership gives market access and processing credibility. Too early for debt — watch list.
notes: Early-stage company — first significant deal only Feb 2026. DRC cross-border sourcing adds complexity. Watch list; revisit in 12–18 months once track record develops.

name: UVAN Ltd
capital_use_of_funds: Seasonal crop purchase working capital
capital_need_source: Claude estimate — one of the largest and longest-established exporters; seasonal working capital is the primary financing need for any vanilla processor at this scale
crs_notes: VANEX founding member alongside Esco and UCIL. One of Uganda's oldest and most established vanilla exporters — operating since 1990 and formerly commanding 60%+ of the Ugandan vanilla market. Named directly alongside CRS in Uganda Ministry of Agriculture vanilla harvest date announcement (2021), indicating a significant sector relationship. President's Exporter Award winner (multiple years including 2009). Family-run by the Sekalala family. No public financial information available but sector stature suggests audited accounts likely exist.
notes: One of the most important sector actors to engage directly. Formerly dominant (60%+ market share historically). Named alongside CRS in government vanilla quality announcement — suggests existing relationship worth exploring. No website beyond VANEX listing.

name: Nillavan Ltd
founded: Unknown (second generation; father started the business)
primary_contact: Cate Nakitanda (Director, Strategy & Operations, Uganda); Ritah Young (Director, Strategy & Marketing, UK)
capital_use_of_funds: Working capital; processing equipment; farmer training support
capital_need_source: Claude estimate — early-to-mid stage operator; Social Vanilla partnership provides market access but not capital
crs_notes: VIEF partner under VINES. VANEX member. Second-generation, female-led Ugandan business — two sisters who took over from their father. Partnership with Social Vanilla (Denmark) for market access and The Sustainable Bean (Denmark) for sector expertise. Anti-middleman positioning and farmer trust-building are core to their model. UK presence (Ritah Young, London) provides international market access. Strong livelihoods and women's empowerment narrative.
notes: Compelling ownership and mission profile — second-generation, female-led, UK/Uganda split, anti-middleman ethos. Social Vanilla (Denmark) is both a market partner and a credibility signal for European buyers. Limited financial profile publicly available. Good CRS alignment on women and livelihoods.

name: Vanaroma Ltd
primary_contact: Mary Musabe Kiddu (Executive Director — 20+ years in Uganda vanilla industry)
capital_use_of_funds: Working capital; processing scale-up; certification costs
crs_notes: Ugandan female-owned exporter with 20+ years sector experience. Premium gourmet positioning with anti-microbial free, slow-cure process targeting minimum 2%+ vanillin content. Named after grandmother Anastazia — strong local roots narrative. Supplies Savory Spice (US retail chain) — documented US buyer relationship is a positive lending signal. Good CRS alignment on women's economic empowerment.
notes: Documented US retail buyer (Savory Spice) is a useful signal of market access and product quality. Executive Director has 20+ years Uganda vanilla experience. Female-owned and operated. Grandmother origin story / brand narrative is distinctive. No public financial information.

name: Mughete Nyakabale Vanilla-Coffee Growers Cooperative Society Ltd
export_markets: Unknown (sells green vanilla beans to buyers)
capital_use_of_funds: Pre-harvest working capital; farmer input finance; aggregation logistics
capital_need_source: Claude estimate — primary producer cooperative selling green beans; working capital for aggregation is the primary financing gap at this level
crs_notes: Farmer-based cooperative registered with Ministry of Trade Industry and Cooperatives (reg. 9611/RCS). Founded 2016 by vanilla farmers facing common problems. 352 members, produces 20MT/year green vanilla beans. Listed on VANEX membership page. Represents the primary producer tier — exactly the type of cooperative CRS's livelihoods mission targets. Needs TA and governance support before debt is appropriate.
notes: Primary producer cooperative — sells green (unprocessed) beans. Represents the upstream end of the value chain. CRS engagement would likely focus on livelihoods and TA before any financial product. Contact via VANEX or CRS VINES team.

name: The Simons Uga Ltd
capital_use_of_funds: Unknown
crs_notes: VANEX member. No public web presence or additional information found. Contact via VANEX directory or CRS VINES team.
notes: Placeholder entry. No public information available beyond VANEX membership listing.

name: Kastus Investments Company Ltd
capital_use_of_funds: Unknown
crs_notes: VANEX member. No public web presence or additional information found. Contact via VANEX directory.
notes: Placeholder entry. No public information available beyond VANEX membership listing.

name: Embe Beverages Ltd
capital_use_of_funds: Unknown
crs_notes: VANEX member. The 'Beverages' name suggests possible downstream processing (vanilla extract, beverages) rather than raw bean export. No public information found to confirm. Contact via VANEX directory.
notes: Placeholder entry. Company name suggests possible beverages/extract processing angle — worth probing. No public information available beyond VANEX membership listing.

name: Kilembe Vanilla Exporters Ltd
hq_location: Kilembe area, western Uganda (implied by name)
capital_use_of_funds: Unknown
crs_notes: VANEX member. Company name suggests Kilembe district base in western Uganda — the Rwenzori foothills area which is a known vanilla growing zone. No public information found. Contact via VANEX directory.
notes: Placeholder entry. Kilembe is in Kasese district, western Uganda — same Rwenzori cluster as Tambiisa, Coetzee, RFCU, and Gourmet Gardens. Geographic clustering may be relevant for a Rwenzori-focused lending approach. No public information beyond VANEX listing.

name: Modern Agro Spices
capital_use_of_funds: Unknown
crs_notes: VANEX member. Company name suggests a broader spice focus beyond vanilla — potentially relevant for a multi-spice lending facility. No public information found. Contact via VANEX directory.
notes: Placeholder entry. Broader spice mandate (not just vanilla) may be relevant given CRS facility is targeting spice sector broadly. No public information beyond VANEX membership listing.
